traits.summary: You can see in dim light and darkness out to 60 feet.
traits.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.

traits.summary: You resist charms and cannot be put to sleep by magic.
traits.description: You have Advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the Charmed condition. You are also immune to magical effects that would put you to sleep.

traits.summary: You gain proficiency in the Perception skill.
traits.description: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

traits.summary: A 4-hour trance grants the benefits of a Long Rest.
traits.description: You do not need to sleep, and magic cannot put you to sleep. You can finish a Long Rest in 4 hours if you spend those hours in a trance-like meditation.

traits.summary: Choose High Elf, Wood Elf, or Drow for additional lineage benefits.
traits.description: Choose a lineage at character creation: High Elf (you know one Wizard cantrip using Intelligence as your spellcasting ability and learn one extra language); Wood Elf (your Speed is 35 feet and you gain Mask of the Wild); Drow (your Darkvision range becomes 120 feet, you know the Dancing Lights cantrip, at 3rd level you can cast Faerie Fire once per Long Rest, and at 5th level you can cast Darkness once per Long Rest; Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells). 2024 note: ability scores are still flexible and chosen separately.

flavorText: Elves are long-lived folk with deep ties to magic, art, and memory. Many move through the world with calm grace shaped by centuries of tradition. Their lineage choices reflect different ancestral paths within elven culture.
roleplayNotes: Decide how your long lifespan shapes your perspective: patient scholar, guarded wanderer, or fiercely protective guardian.
